purpose: Third AS-owned schematic-linked Universal Cell trace, covering the stem automail subset that reconfigures a stem cell into a processor-left cell.

ports.label: north
ports.summary: First signal channel on the triangular stem node.

ports.label: east
ports.summary: Second signal channel on the triangular stem node.

ports.label: west
ports.summary: Third signal channel on the triangular stem node.

layers.summary: A pl automail command reconfigures the stem node into a processor with left memory and consumes the automail marker.
layers.boundary: This layer states symbolic stem automail reconfiguration only; it does not assert full PRC buffer processing.

layers.summary: The stem node is represented using the same triangular GELC/RLEM port vocabulary as the fixed-role traces.
layers.boundary: This layer fixes schematic geometry and port orientation, not a complete dynamic GELC reconfiguration circuit.

layers.summary: The trace records every current AS Cell field before and after one stem automail activation.
layers.boundary: This layer is limited to the existing AS stem automail subset and adds no new transition semantics.

layers.summary: Switchable circulator hardware and auxiliary reconfiguration support remain candidate interpretations, not verified AS hardware.
layers.boundary: This layer keeps physical implementation and RALA-like auxiliary circuitry separate from the tested automail subset.
